steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Mercer and Price hypothesized that macroalgae negatively affect MPB biomass. Their actual result: no significant correlation between benthic chlorophyll and macroalgal mat abundance. The blank must draw a conclusion that follows logically from that null result.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — their hypothesis about the relationship between macroalgae and MPB was not supported and their finding was instead consistent with that of Choudhury and colleagues.: No correlation means no negative effect — the hypothesis of a negative relationship is not supported. This also aligns with Choudhury and colleagues, who similarly found macroalgae do not impact MPB. Both conditions are met. ✓
  • Choice B — although this finding was consistent with that of Ortega and Morales, trends in MPB biomass are likely not a result of stresses caused by macroalgae.: Gets the comparison backwards. Ortega and Morales found that benthic chlorophyll decreased with macroalgae — a negative relationship. Mercer and Price found no correlation, which contradicts Ortega and Morales, not confirms it. ✗
  • Choice C — differences between their study design and that of Ortega and Morales likely explain the discrepancy in the two studies' findings about the responses of MPB to macroalgal mats.: Invents an explanation for the discrepancy. The passage provides no information about study design differences; this inference is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice D — rather than inhibiting chlorophyll production in MPB, as they had hypothesized, macroalgal mats may positively affect chlorophyll production.: No correlation means no relationship in either direction. Inferring a positive effect from a null result is an unwarranted leap. ✗
That is the correct answer.
